entries.summary: Students at Dhaka University began protests after court reinstated job quota system.
Protesters blocked major roads in nationwide Bangla Blockade demonstrations across cities.
Hasina compared protesters to razakars at a press conference, inflaming public anger.
Ruling party youth wing attacked protesters at Dhaka University; dozens wounded in clashes.
Student killed at Begum Rokeya University in Rangpur became iconic protest moment.
Video showed protesters being ambushed by gunfire at Dhaka University campus.
Authorities cut internet nationwide as eleven more protesters were reportedly killed.
Government imposed nationwide curfew and reported shoot-on-sight orders.
Supreme Court scaled back government job quota to five percent.
Al Jazeera explained how peaceful protests morphed into nationwide unrest.
Students issued a one-point demand for Hasina's immediate resignation.
Nearly 100 people killed in the single deadliest day of protests.
Hasina resigned and fled to India as crowds stormed her official residence.
World leaders publicly reacted to Hasina's removal from power in Bangladesh.
Al Jazeera analysed how Gen Z protests brought down a 15-year rule.
Yunus said interim government needed time before holding fresh national elections.
Anniversary coverage examined an unresolved revolution one year after Hasina's fall.
CNN reported on Bangladesh protests as the death toll mounted amid crackdown.
Universities shut down across Bangladesh as violence escalated nationwide that week.
CNN explainer described the deepening quota protest crisis and government response.
Bangladesh Supreme Court rolled back the controversial public job quotas.
Students issued one-point demand for Hasina to resign as protests intensified.
Police-protester clashes killed at least 91 in single deadliest day of unrest.
Hasina resigned and fled to India by helicopter after weeks of unrest.
Crowds stormed and looted Hasina's official Dhaka residence after her departure.
Bangladesh president dissolved parliament and ordered Khaleda Zia's release from custody.
Yunus sworn in as chief adviser of the new interim government.
Students rallied at vandalised Hasina mural demanding accountability for killings.
Yunus's inspection revealed secret Aynaghar detention centers under former government.
Bangladesh court issued an arrest warrant for former prime minister Hasina.
Thousands rallied at the March for Unity in Dhaka marking the uprising.
Activists stormed and demolished founding leader Mujibur Rahman's historic Dhaka home.
UN human rights office reported up to 1,400 killed during the crackdown.
